title: Archer vs. Joby: The eVTOL Race Just Got Real -- Here's Which Stock Wins
text: Both Archer Aviation and Joby Aviation are advancing their electric vertical takeoff and landing aircraft toward commercialization in 2026. Archer has completed the third stage of FAA certification, while Joby has made significant progress in the final certification stage by flying an FAA-conforming aircraft. Joby is further along operationally with a vertical integration strategy planning to manufacture components internally and operate an air-taxi service similar to Uber, potentially yielding higher long-term profit margins despite higher upfront R&D costs. Archer outsources manufacturing to established suppliers like Honeywell and Molicel for batteries, which may accelerate short-term progress but provides less supply chain control. The analyst recommends Joby as the better choice for aggressive investors, citing its operational lead and superior business model. However, both stocks carry substantial risk, with no guarantee that the eVTOL concept will achieve commercial success. Joby trades with a $10B market cap at $10.37 while Archer has a $4.9B market cap trading at $6.05. Both companies currently operate at significant losses.
